overview: The Properties Filter processes Java `.properties` files, extracting key-value pairs as translatable text units. It is based on the `java.util.Properties` specification and supports additional comment styles for compatibility with other properties-like formats. The filter supports localization directives, key-based filtering, sub-filters for embedded content (e.g., HTML), and inline code detection via regex patterns.

identification.useLd.help:
Enables recognition of localization directives — special comments that override default extraction behavior. When disabled, any directives in the input are ignored.

Localization directives have **precedence over key conditions**. Their syntax is shared across all Okapi filters.
notes: Directives override key conditions when both are active.

extraction.localizeOutside.help:
Controls whether items **not** within the scope of a localization directive are extracted.

- **Enabled**: items outside directive scope are extracted (use directives only to *exclude*)
- **Disabled**: only items explicitly within directive scope are extracted (use directives to *include*)

This lets you mark up fewer parts of the source — choose the minority case for your directives.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when localization directives are enabled

extraction.useKeyCondition.help:
Enables filtering of entries based on their property key. When enabled, only entries whose keys match (or don't match) the `keyCondition` regex are extracted.

Localization directives take precedence over key conditions.
notes: Localization directives have precedence over key conditions.

extraction.extractOnlyMatchingKey.help:
Controls the key filtering behavior:

- **true** — Extract **only** items with keys matching the pattern
- **false** — Extract all items **except** those with keys matching the pattern

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when key condition is enabled

extraction.keyCondition.help:
Java regular expression tested against property keys to determine extraction.

The pattern uses `java.util.regex.Pattern` syntax.
examples: `^(?!internal\.).*` — matches all keys except those starting with "internal."

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when key condition is enabled

extraction.extraComments.help:
Recognizes additional comment styles beyond strict Java properties comments (`#` and `!`):

- Lines starting with `;`
- Lines where `//` is the first non-whitespace sequence

`//` after a `=` is treated as part of the value, not a comment.

notes.commentsAreNotes.help:
Attaches comments preceding each entry as a **note** property on the corresponding text unit. All comment lines before an entry are grouped into a single note.

Useful for providing translators with context about the purpose of each string.

identification.idLikeResname.help:
Uses the property key as the text unit ID. Note that the key is **already** extracted into the `name` property (`resname` in XLIFF) regardless of this setting.

Some output formats have restrictions on ID values — for example, XLIFF 2.0 requires IDs to be NMTOKEN values, which property keys may not satisfy.
notes: The key is always available as `resname` regardless of this setting.
Property keys may not be valid IDs for all output formats (e.g., XLIFF2 requires NMTOKEN).

output.escapeExtendedChars.help:
Converts all characters above U+007F to Unicode escape sequences (`\uHHHH`) in the output.

When disabled, only characters not supported by the output encoding are escaped. Enable this for strict Java properties compatibility where the file must be ASCII-safe.

output.useJavaEscapes.help: Retains Java property escape sequences in extracted text rather than converting them. When disabled (default), escapes are resolved to their actual characters during extraction.

output.convertLFandTab.help:
Converts `\n` and `\t` escape sequences to actual line-breaks and tabs during processing. All other escaped characters (e.g., `\\`, `\:`, `\=`) remain escaped.

Disable this if your properties values use `\n` and `\t` as literal text rather than control characters.

inlineCodes.enabled.help:
Enables pattern-based detection of inline codes in extracted text. Matches are converted to inline code placeholders, protecting them from translation.

The default patterns detect:
- `printf`-style format specifiers (`%s`, `%d`, `%2$s`)
- Escape sequences (`\n`, `\r`, `\t`, etc.)
- Java `MessageFormat` placeholders (`{0}`, `{1,date}`)

processingNotes: BOM handling: if the input has a Unicode BOM, the corresponding encoding is used. Output BOM is only preserved for UTF-8 → UTF-8 conversions where the input had a BOM.
Line-breaks in the output match the original input format.
Each property entry maps to one text unit: key → name (`resname`), value → source content, preceding comments → note (if enabled).
Java properties in XML format should be processed with the XML Filter or XML Stream Filter, not this filter.

examples.title: Basic properties file
examples.description: A simple Java properties file showing the two key-value separator styles. Both `=` and `:` are supported.
examples.input:
```properties
# This is a comment

labelOK= OK
msgBadFile: Invalid input file
```

examples.title: Key-based filtering
examples.description: Using the key condition `.*text.*` with **Extract only matching keys** enabled. Only keys containing "text" are extracted for translation.
examples.input:
```properties
key1 = Text for key1
text.err1 = Text for text.err1
menu_text_file = Text for menu_text_file
```

examples.title: HTML sub-filter configuration
examples.description: Using the `okf_properties-html-subfilter` configuration to process property values containing HTML markup through the HTML filter.

useLd.title: Use Localization Directives
useLd.description: Enable recognition of localization directives in comments to override default extraction behavior.

localizeOutside.title: Extract Outside Directives
localizeOutside.description: Extract translatable items that are not within the scope of a localization directive.

useKeyCondition.title: Enable Key Condition
useKeyCondition.description: Filter entries for extraction based on a regex condition applied to their keys.

extractOnlyMatchingKey.title: Extract Only Matching Keys
extractOnlyMatchingKey.description: When enabled, extract only entries whose keys match the condition; otherwise exclude matching keys.

keyCondition.title: Key Condition Pattern
keyCondition.description: Regular expression pattern to test against property keys for extraction filtering.

extraComments.title: Additional Comment Markers
extraComments.description: Recognize semicolon and double-slash comment styles in addition to standard # and ! markers.

commentsAreNotes.title: Comments as Notes
commentsAreNotes.description: Attach preceding comments to each entry as translator notes.

idLikeResname.title: Use Key as Text Unit ID
idLikeResname.description: Use the property key value as the text unit ID in addition to the resource name.

escapeExtendedChars.title: Escape Extended Characters
escapeExtendedChars.description: Convert all characters above U+007F to \uHHHH escape sequences in the output.

useJavaEscapes.title: Retain Java Escapes
useJavaEscapes.description: Keep Java property escape sequences in extracted text instead of resolving them.

convertLFandTab.title: Convert \n and \t
convertLFandTab.description: Convert \n and \t escape sequences to actual line-breaks and tabs during processing.

fullDoc:
# Properties Filter

The Properties Filter processes Java `.properties` files, extracting key-value pairs as translatable text units. It is based on the `java.util.Properties` specification and supports additional comment styles for compatibility with other properties-like formats. The filter supports localization directives, key-based filtering, sub-filters for embedded content (e.g., HTML), and inline code detection via regex patterns.

Java properties can also be represented in XML — use the XML Filter or XML Stream Filter for those.

## Parameters

### Extraction

#### Enable Key Condition (`useKeyCondition`)
Enables filtering of entries based on their property key. When enabled, only entries whose keys match (or don't match) the `keyCondition` regex are extracted. Localization directives take precedence over key conditions.

#### Extract Only Matching Keys (`extractOnlyMatchingKey`)
Controls the key filtering behavior:
- **true** — Extract **only** items with keys matching the pattern
- **false** — Extract all items **except** those with keys matching the pattern

Only meaningful when the key condition is enabled.

#### Key Condition Pattern (`keyCondition`)
Java regular expression tested against property keys. Uses `java.util.regex.Pattern` syntax.

Examples:
- `.*text.*` — matches keys containing "text"
- `^msg\.` — matches keys starting with "msg."

#### Extract Outside Directives (`localizeOutside`)
Controls whether items not within the scope of a localization directive are extracted. Only meaningful when localization directives are enabled.

#### Additional Comment Markers (`extraComments`)
Recognizes comment styles beyond `#` and `!`:
- Lines starting with `;`
- Lines where `//` is the first non-whitespace sequence

Note: `//` after `=` is treated as part of the value, not a comment.

### Notes

#### Comments as Notes (`commentsAreNotes`)
Attaches comments preceding each entry as a note on the corresponding text unit. All comment lines before an entry are grouped into a single note.

### Identification

#### Use Key as Text Unit ID (`idLikeResname`)
Uses the property key as the text unit ID. The key is already extracted into the `name` property (`resname` in XLIFF) regardless. Property keys may not be valid IDs for all output formats (e.g., XLIFF2 requires NMTOKEN). Available since M31.

#### Use Localization Directives (`useLd`)
Enables recognition of localization directives — special comments that override default extraction behavior. Directives override key conditions when both are active.

### Output

#### Escape Extended Characters (`escapeExtendedChars`)
Converts all characters above U+007F to `\uHHHH` escape sequences. When disabled, only characters unsupported by the output encoding are escaped.

#### Retain Java Escapes (`useJavaEscapes`)
Retains Java property escape sequences in extracted text rather than converting them to actual characters.

#### Convert \\n and \\t (`convertLFandTab`)
Converts `\n` and `\t` escape sequences to actual line-breaks and tabs. All other escaped characters remain escaped.

### Inline Codes

#### Enable Inline Codes (`inlineCodes.enabled`)
Enables pattern-based detection of inline codes. Matches are converted to placeholders protected from translation. Default patterns detect printf-style specifiers, escape sequences, and MessageFormat placeholders.

#### Code Finder Rules (`inlineCodes.rules`)
Regex patterns identifying inline codes in translatable text.

#### Merge Adjacent (`inlineCodes.mergeAdjacent`)
Merge consecutive inline codes into a single placeholder.

#### Move Boundary Codes (`inlineCodes.moveBoundaryCodes`)
Move inline codes at segment boundaries to the skeleton (non-translatable portion).

## Limitations

None known.

## Notes

- BOM handling: if the input has a Unicode BOM, the corresponding encoding is used. Output BOM is only preserved for UTF-8 → UTF-8 conversions where the input had a BOM.
- Line-breaks in the output match the original input format.
- Each property entry maps to one text unit: key → name (`resname`), value → source content, preceding comments → note (if enabled).

## Examples

### Basic properties file

```properties
# This is a comment

labelOK= OK
msgBadFile: Invalid input file
```

### Key-based filtering

Using `keyCondition: ".*text.*"` with extract-only-matching enabled:

```properties
key1 = Text for key1
text.err1 = Text for text.err1
menu_text_file = Text for menu_text_file
```

Result: only `text.err1` and `menu_text_file` are extracted.
